commit.message:
feat: MED17 platform support — full implementation, documentation, tests, and example data

Add complete MED17 ECU platform support alongside existing ME7 workflows.
This is the full MED17 implementation for the Audi RS3/TTRS 2.5T TFSI
(MED17.1.62) and compatible platforms (4.0T, 5.2 V10).

=== MED17 Core Implementation ===

Data Layer:
- Med17LogParser: ScorpionEFI CSV log parser with header extraction
  from 'Description(signal_name)(unit)' format, supports OPTIMIZER
  and LDRPID log types
- Med17LogAdapter: translates MED17 signal names to ME7 equivalents
  (psrg_w→pvdks_w, pvds_w→pssol_w, pu_w→pus_w, tvldste_w→ldtvm)
  enabling shared analysis algorithms
- Med17LogHeaderPreference: persistent preference storage for MED17
  log header configuration (parallel to ME7 LogHeaderPreference)
- Med17LogFileContract: 28 signal constants, 23 Header enum entries
  covering core engine, boost control, lambda/fuel trim, dual injection,
  VE model, and gear/voltage signals

=== Dual Injection (MED17-Only Feature) ===

The 2.5T runs port + direct injection simultaneously. New support for:
- KRKTE_PFI / KRKTE_GDI: separate injector constant calculations
- TVUB_PFI: port injector dead time characterization
- InjectorScalingSolver: Bernoulli pressure correction for injector
  swaps across different fuel pressures (4 bar PFI vs 200+ bar GDI)
- Fuel split calculator: port vs direct share at load/RPM points
- DualInjectionPreferences: persistent storage for injector specs
- DualInjectionScreen reads from prefs, persists on calculation

=== Profile System Parity ===

- ConfigurationProfile: added med17LogHeaders field
- MED17 bundled profile (MED17_162_RS3_TTRS_2_5T): all 30 ScorpionEFI
  header mappings populated, dual injection config (220cc port @4bar,
  160cc direct @200bar, 30% port share, 5-cylinder), EA855 EVO primary
  fueling (0.496L displacement, 5 cyl, 2.48L total)
- ProfileManager.applyProfile(): now handles MED17 log headers via
  Med17LogHeaderPreference and dual injection prefs
- ProfileManager.exportCurrentProfile(): captures MED17 headers and
  dual injection config for profile round-trip

=== Documentation ===

README.md (+192 lines):
- MED17 intro, supported ECUs table (RS3/TTRS, 4.0T, V10)
- 'How MED17 Works' section (torque-based architecture comparison)
- Platform selection, MED17 unit assumptions, ScorpionEFI log headers
- ME7 vs MED17 comparison table (injection, MAF, VE model, signals)
- Dual Injection section (port/direct/split calculator)
- MED17 notes on all shared calibration sections (KFMIOP→KFLMIOP,
  KFMIRL→KFLMIRL, LDRPID signal names, Optimizer signal mapping)
- ME7-only sections clearly marked (MLHFM, KFVPDKSD, WDKUGDN, Alpha-N)

calibration-guide.md (+82 lines):
- Dual Injection step-by-step workflow (port/direct/TVUB/split)
- MED17 PLSOL notes (fupsrls_w ≈ 0.091 for EA855 EVO)
- MED17 LDRPID notes with ScorpionEFI signal mapping table

ScreenshotHarness.kt (+88 lines):
- MED17 screenshot captures: dual injection, configuration, fueling,
  KFMIOP, KFMIRL, LDRPID, PLSOL, optimizer
- Platform switching (ME7→MED17→restore) for idempotent generation

=== Example Data Reorganization ===

Restructured example/ directory by platform:
- example/me7/bin/    — ME7 binary files
- example/me7/xdf/    — ME7 XDF definition files
- example/me7/kp/     — ME7 WinOLS KP files
- example/me7/logs/   — 416 ME7Logger CSV log files
- example/med17/logs/ — 3 ScorpionEFI CSV log files (DS1 MED17.1.62)

=== Housekeeping ===

- technical/ directory added to .gitignore (proprietary Funktionsrahmen)

=== Test Suite (323 tests, 0 failures) ===

16 new test files across all layers:

Parser tests:
- Med17LogParserTest: header extraction, signal parsing, multi-file
  directory loading, edge cases (empty files, missing signals)
- Med17LogAdapterTest: signal name translation, optimizer/LDRPID
  format conversion
- Me7LogParserTest: existing ME7 parser regression tests
- AfrLogParserTest: wideband AFR log parser tests

Domain model tests:
- KrkteCalculatorTest: primary fueling formula validation
- InjectorScalingSolverTest (21): Bernoulli correction, pressure
  scaling, TVUB dead time, edge cases
- OptimizerCalculatorMe7Test: ME7 optimizer workflow validation
- OptimizerCalculatorMed17Test: MED17 optimizer with signal adaptation
- OptimizerMapSuggestionTest (36): KFLDRL/KFLDIMX suggestion
  generation, multi-log merging, edge cases
- DetectorTest (38): shared boost error, VE deviation, intervention
  detection unit tests
- ChainDiagnosisTest (24): detector chain orchestration
- PidSimulatorTest (32): PID simulation accuracy
- PlsolTest + PlsolMed17Test (9): pressure-to-load for both platforms
- LdrpidCalculatorTest (6): feed-forward PID linearization

Writer tests:
- BinWriterTest: 17 E2E tests (XDF→BIN→write→verify byte isolation)
  + 25 inverse equation tests (formula inversion for all patterns)

Profile tests:
- ProfileManagerTest (17): MED17 header population, dual injection
  config, ME7 regression, JSON round-trip, bundled profile integrity

commit.message:
kernel: add patch 0005 to fix WiFi CLK_ENABLE after hardware reset

The A80 MMC controller's CLKCR register defaults to 0 at power-on.
Channels never touched by U-Boot (mmc1 = BCM4330 WiFi SDIO) keep
CLKCR=0 through probe, causing the first UPCLK_ONLY command to hang
forever (fatal err update clk timeout).

Fix by writing SDXC_CARD_CLOCK_ON into CLKCR after sunxi_mmc_reset_host()
in sunxi_mmc_init_host(), mirroring U-Boot's existing workaround.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
uboot: boot eMMC directly before distro scan to avoid mmc0 init

On the A80 a failed voltage-select negotiation on mmc0 (plain SD card)
corrupts the shared mmc_config_clk state and prevents mmc dev 1 (eMMC)
from initialising.  The distro_bootcmd probes mmc0 first even when SPL
booted from eMMC (mmc_bootdev=1), so an empty SD card in the slot
reliably prevents booting from eMMC.

Override CONFIG_BOOTCOMMAND to attempt eMMC (mmc 1) directly and
unconditionally before running distro_bootcmd.  This ensures mmc0 is
never probed when eMMC has a valid kernel, preventing the shared-state
corruption.  The distro_bootcmd fallback still handles initial SD-card
installs (empty eMMC) transparently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
mmc: sunxi: skip WAIT_PRE_OVER when mask_data0 is set

Fix WiFi (BCM4330 SDIO) failing to initialise when an SD card is
simultaneously present in the CubieBoard4 slot.

SDXC_MASK_DATA0 in CLKCR bypasses the DATA0 idle check for the
clock-configuration register write, but SDXC_WAIT_PRE_OVER in CMDR
re-introduces that same DATA0 check for the subsequent clock-update
command.  On A80 the CMDR check is not suppressed by MASK_DATA0, so
the oclk_onoff loop always hits the 750 ms deadline when BCM4330 is
in reset (DATA0 stuck low), returning -EIO and preventing card init.

When mask_data0 is set for a host, omit SDXC_WAIT_PRE_OVER from the
UPCLK_ONLY command.  Clock updates then complete immediately even
with DATA0 held low, matching the documented intent of mask_data0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

boot: prefer eMMC over SD when both are bootable

When the A80 hardware ROM finds a valid SPL on the SD card,
it boots from SD (mmc_bootdev=0) and U-Boot scans SD first.
If the SD card is a stale CB4 image with boot.scr, it would
boot from the card instead of eMMC.

Fix: at the top of boot.cmd, when devnum=0 (SD), try to load
the DTB from eMMC (mmc 1).  If that succeeds, load the kernel
from eMMC too and boot with root=/dev/mmcblk2p2, redirecting
away from the SD image.  If eMMC has no kernel yet (fresh
install workflow), the check fails silently and SD boot
proceeds as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace API key auth with self-signed EdDSA JWTs (#62)

* feat: replace API key auth with self-signed EdDSA JWTs

Replace the SHA-256 hashed API key authentication system with self-signed
Ed25519 (EdDSA) JWTs. This establishes an open authentication protocol
shared between the bitrouter CLI and the cloud service.

Core changes:
- Add bitrouter-core/src/jwt/ module: claims types (BitrouterClaims,
  TokenScope, BudgetScope, BudgetRange), Ed25519 key management
  (MasterKeypair, MasterKeyJson), and manual JWT sign/verify/decode
- Manual JWT construction (base64url header.payload.signature) using
  ed25519-dalek directly, avoiding jsonwebtoken crate compatibility issues

Account system:
- New migration: add master_pubkey to accounts, create rotated_pubkeys
  table for key rotation history, drop api_keys table
- Replace API key entity with rotated_pubkey entity
- AccountService: find_by_pubkey, create_with_pubkey,
  find_or_create_by_pubkey (auto-registration), rotate_pubkey
- Account filters: single GET /accounts/me (account-scoped)

Auth & server:
- Rewrite auth.rs: JwtAuthContext with security-critical ordering
  (decode -> verify signature -> check exp -> DB lookup)
- Signature verified BEFORE any DB interaction to prevent
  unauthenticated account creation
- Admin scope is account-relative, not global server admin
- Open mode (no DB) preserves unauthenticated local proxy access
- Remove master_key from config, remove old key management endpoints

CLI commands:
- bitrouter account: generate Ed25519 keypairs, list, set active
- bitrouter keygen: sign JWTs with active master key (scope, exp,
  models, budget options)
- bitrouter keys: list/show/rm locally-stored JWTs
- Keys stored at BITROUTER_HOME/.keys/<pubkey_prefix>/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- New migration: add master_pubkey to accounts, create rotated_pubkeys
  table for key rotation history, drop api_keys table
- Replace API key entity with rotated_pubkey entity
- AccountService: find_by_pubkey, create_with_pubkey,
  find_or_create_by_pubkey (auto-registration), rotate_pubkey
- Account filters: single GET /accounts/me (account-scoped)

Auth & server:
- Rewrite auth.rs: JwtAuthContext with security-critical ordering
  (decode -> verify signature -> check exp -> DB lookup)
- Signature verified BEFORE any DB interaction to prevent
  unauthenticated account creation
- Admin scope is account-relative, not global server admin
- Open mode (no DB) preserves unauthenticated local proxy access
- Remove master_key from config, remove old key management endpoints

CLI commands:
- bitrouter account: generate Ed25519 keypairs, list, set active
- bitrouter keygen: sign JWTs with active master key (scope, exp,
  models, budget options)
- bitrouter keys: list/show/rm locally-stored JWTs
- Keys stored at BITROUTER_HOME/.keys/<pubkey_prefix>/

feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()
- context_agent/adapters/ltm_adapter.py: +agentic_search() with AgenticRetriever fallback
- context_agent/orchestration/context_aggregator.py: +mode/max_level/category_filter params
- context_agent/api/schemas.py: +ContextUsedRequest/Response, +ToolResultRequest/Response
- context_agent/api/http_handler.py: +POST /context/used, +POST /tools/result
- context_agent/api/router.py: +mark_used(), +record_tool_result() facade methods
- tests/unit/test_openviking_capabilities.py: 38 new unit tests (all passing)
- docs/architecture-design.md: +Appendix D with capability details
- docs/agent-integration-guide.md: +Section 9 with usage guide

* feat: inventory allocation system

* Add inventory sync endpoints, task APIs & UI

Introduce inventory snapshot/delta endpoints and related client sync logic, plus job task APIs and UI improvements. Key changes:

- New API routes: /api/inventory/items/snapshot and /api/inventory/items/delta to support initial snapshots and incremental deltas (with change threshold & caching).
- Allocations/transactions: allocations GET supports with_relations and limit; transactions GET supports limit and returns recent transactions with item names.
- Estimate endpoints updated to use the inventory allocation service: adding/updating/removing estimate items now integrates with allocations and returns allocation/stock info; InsufficientStockError handled with 409 responses.
- Job task APIs: added CRUD routes for tasks and a dedicated status endpoint with validation, rate limiting, and inventory side effects (reserve, consume, release). New task domain/repository files and a migration for job card tasks/inventory delta.
- Inventory page UI: show On Hand / Reserved / Available columns, reserved stock and recent activity cards, fetches allocations and transactions, and refresh behavior after adjustments.
- Misc: moved script imports to src paths, added jest.setup.d.ts, new hooks (use-inventory-snapshot, use-job-tasks), and many repository/use-case updates to support allocation flows.

This commit ties inventory reservation/consumption into estimates and job tasks, adds sync endpoints for efficient client caching, and surfaces reservations/activity in the tenant inventory UI.

- New API routes: /api/inventory/items/snapshot and /api/inventory/items/delta to support initial snapshots and incremental deltas (with change threshold & caching).
- Allocations/transactions: allocations GET supports with_relations and limit; transactions GET supports limit and returns recent transactions with item names.
- Estimate endpoints updated to use the inventory allocation service: adding/updating/removing estimate items now integrates with allocations and returns allocation/stock info; InsufficientStockError handled with 409 responses.
- Job task APIs: added CRUD routes for tasks and a dedicated status endpoint with validation, rate limiting, and inventory side effects (reserve, consume, release). New task domain/repository files and a migration for job card tasks/inventory delta.
- Inventory page UI: show On Hand / Reserved / Available columns, reserved stock and recent activity cards, fetches allocations and transactions, and refresh behavior after adjustments.
- Misc: moved script imports to src paths, added jest.setup.d.ts, new hooks (use-inventory-snapshot, use-job-tasks), and many repository/use-case updates to support allocation flows.

This commit ties inventory reservation/consumption into estimates and job tasks, adds sync endpoints for efficient client caching, and surfaces reservations/activity in the tenant inventory UI.

commit.message:
feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and
auto-implement steps with a recursive fs.readdirSync('lua') call.
This keeps codebase context up to date automatically as new Lua
modules are added without requiring workflow changes.

Previously only impact < 4 triggered the label. Now type=other always
gets 'evaluation needed' regardless of impact score, matching the
intended behaviour that unclassified issues always need manual review.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

commit.message:
Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test execution (#114)

* Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test support

Implement thread-local dispatch for ARM32 architecture, matching the
existing x86_64 and aarch64 implementations. This enables parallel test
execution on ARM32 Linux targets by replacing the global mutex approach
with per-thread function replacement routing.

Changes:
- Add target_arch = "arm" to all thread-local dispatch cfg gates across
  thread_local_registry.rs, internal.rs, injector.rs, common.rs, and
  thread_safety.rs
- Implement ARM32 dispatcher JIT code generation (ARM mode) with
  register save/restore for both integer (r0-r3) and VFP (d0-d7) regs
- Add ARM32 trampoline creation with support for both ARM and Thumb mode
  jump-back sequences
- Add PC-relative instruction fixup for ARM mode trampolines
- Add ARM32 branch patch generation matching existing patch_arm.rs format
  including Thumb alignment handling
- Add ARM32 JIT code for boolean return values (MOV r0, #imm; BX lr)
- Enable allocate_jit_memory for ARM32 (uses simple mmap fallback)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ARM32 dispatcher stack alignment and patch register clobbering

- Fix AAPCS stack alignment violation: PUSH {r0-r3, r4, lr} (24 bytes,
  8-aligned) instead of PUSH {r0-r3, lr} (20 bytes, misaligned).
  Misaligned SP before BLX caused SIGABRT on armv7.

- Use r12 (IP, scratch register) instead of r7 (callee-saved/frame pointer)
  for Thumb branch patch. r7 clobbering corrupted the caller's frame pointer,
  causing SIGSEGV on thumbv7. Uses MOVW/MOVT r12 encoding which is also
  alignment-independent.

- Use r12 instead of r9 (callee-saved) for ARM branch patch.

* Reduce ARM32 test stress and add diagnostics for CI

- Add RUST_BACKTRACE=full for ARM target CI runs
- Run ARM tests with --test-threads=1 to avoid resource exhaustion
  on 32-bit compat runners (armv7 was hitting thread spawn limits)
- Reduce stress test THREAD_COUNT from 20 to 4 on ARM32

* Fix ARM32 function overflow with dynamic 4-byte patch size

Use dynamic patch sizing for ARM32: 4-byte B.W (Thumb) or B (ARM) when
the dispatcher is within branch range (±16MB/±32MB), falling back to
12-byte MOVW/MOVT/BX when it is not. This eliminates the previous
12-byte patch overflow into adjacent small functions that caused
SIGSEGV on thumbv7.

Key changes:
- Add ARM32 to constrained JIT allocation (±16MB range) in common.rs
- Pre-allocate dispatcher, then choose patch size based on distance
- For Thumb 4-byte patches, prepend a Thumb-to-ARM stub (LDR.W/BX)
  since Thumb B.W cannot switch processor mode
- Encode Thumb B.W (T4) and ARM B (unconditional) for 4-byte patches
- Add -j 1 to CI cargo test for ARM targets to prevent resource
  exhaustion from parallel test binary execution

* Add diagnostics for thumbv7 SIGSEGV in test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread

Add eprintln diagnostics to:
- install_dispatcher_arm32: log addresses, distance, patch size
- generate_branch_patch_arm32: verify B.W encoding round-trips correctly
- test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread: log each step to identify crash point

Also add isolated test run CI step for ARM targets to determine if the
crash is caused by accumulated state from prior tests.

* Fix ARM32 Thumb overlapping patch SIGILL and instruction boundary bugs

- Use std::hint::black_box in test helper functions to ensure minimum
  4-byte code size on Thumb (prevents compiler from generating 2-byte
  functions that cause overlapping patches)
- Add calculate_thumb_copy_size() to scan Thumb instructions and find
  the first complete instruction boundary >= patch_size bytes, preventing
  the trampoline from splitting a 32-bit Thumb instruction
- Add check_arm32_patch_overlap() to detect overlapping patches at
  runtime with clear diagnostic messages (only checks active patches
  with ref_count > 0)
- Remove diagnostic eprintln statements and isolated test CI step

consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality.

1. **Enhanced Clarity**

`str.endsWith("/")`
- Replaced `substring(0, str.length - 1)` with more concise `slice(0,
-1)`

URL construction

2. **Improved Consistency**

slash removal

   - All changes are purely stylistic - no behavioral changes
   - Logic flow remains identical
   - Same error handling and edge cases

- #18047 - Add support for versioned CDN URLs in Tools and Transcoder
classes
- Commit 793d073 - Add support for versioned CDN URLs

````typescript
const baseUrl = Tools.ScriptBaseUrl[Tools.ScriptBaseUrl.length - 1] === "/"
````
